title: Ilya Sutskever
excerpts:
Section Title: Conversation
Content:
Ilya Sutskever
@ilyasut
It’s extremely good that Anthropic has not backed down, and it’s siginficant that OpenAI has taken a similar stance. In the future, there will be much more challenging situations of this nature, and it will be critical for the relevant leaders to rise up to the occasion, for fierce competitors to put their differences aside. Good to see that happen today.

title: The Robot Brains: OpenAI's Ilya Sutskever
excerpts:
Section Title: The Robot Brains: OpenAI's Ilya Sutskever
Content:
The Industry
September 21, 2021
Pieter Abbeel
Co-Founder & Chief Scientist
Share
[Share to Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=) [Share to Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=) Share via Email [Share to LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=)
Copied!
Copy Link
The Robot Brains: OpenAI's Ilya Sutskever
On the last episode of Season One, our guest is [Ilya Sutskever](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilya_Sutskever) . Ilya is the Co-Founder and Chief Scientist of [OpenAI](https://openai.com/) . As a PhD student at Toronto, Ilya was one of the authors on the [2012 AlexNet paper](https://papers.nips.cc/paper/2012/hash/c399862d3b9d6b76c8436e924a68c45b-Abstract.html) that completely changed the field of AI, resulting in the widespread adoption of deep learning and the avalanche of AI breakthroughs we’ve seen the past 10 years.

title: AI 2026: Ilya Sutskever and the End of the Age of Scaling
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > The researcher who played a central role in developing ChatGPT now bets that new fundamenta...
Content:
Ilya Sutskever is one of the world’s leading AI scientists. As of December 2025, he is the CEO of Safe Superintelligence (SSI), a startup he co-founded upon his departure from OpenAI in May 2024, where he was also a co-founder and worked for over eight years as Chief Scientist. Under his technical leadership, OpenAI, which began as a small non-profit, became the world’s leading generative AI company with a sizable lead over its competitors in both technology and paying user base, a lead it still retains today.

title: welcome · Sutskever's List - liveBook · Manning
excerpts:
Section Title: welcome
Content:
The book takes you inside a curated set of research papers known informally as “ *Sutskever’s List,* ” which Ilya Sutskever once described to John Carmack as covering “90% of what matters today” in AI. However, instead of viewing each paper as a separate contribution, the chapters depict them as interconnected threads within the broader narrative they represent. You will see how technical breakthroughs intersect with organizational decisions, safety debates, and cultural inflection points, and how they continue to shape the systems we use today.
